dialogueChunks.intro: Our rivals moved faster than I expected, {player_name}. The game has changed.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {target_npc} discovered what we're planning and has started rallying opposition. They've been spreading word through {location} that our efforts to secure {quest_focus} are a threat to everyone. Meanwhile, the {enemy_type} activity near {location_hint} has cut off our supply lines, and without {quest_item} we can't finish what we started. I need you to break through the blockade and bring back enough {quest_item} to tip the balance before {target_npc} turns the whole settlement against us.
dialogueChunks.outro: If we lose momentum now, {quest_stakes} slips through our fingers and we'll never get another chance.

playerResponses.text: Blockades are meant to be broken. I'll punch through.
Rival factions and monsters. This escalation will cost you extra.
The opposition is too strong. I'm cutting my losses.

dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, someone tried to burn my workshop last night. This is getting personal.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {target_npc} has hired mercenaries to intimidate anyone supporting our claim to {quest_focus}. Three of my allies in {location} have already backed down under pressure. I need you to find the mercenary camp {location_hint}, scatter them, and recover the {quest_item} they stole from my stores. Without those supplies, I can't fulfill the promises I've made. If my supporters see me fail to deliver, they'll abandon me for {target_npc} without a second thought.
dialogueChunks.outro: This isn't just about {quest_stakes} anymore. It's about survival. Mine and everyone who bet on me.

playerResponses.text: Mercenaries and arsonists. I'll deal with both.
Your enemies are escalating to violence. This is getting dangerous.
Hired thugs are a line I won't cross in either direction.

dialogueChunks.intro: The situation has become more complicated than I imagined, {player_name}. But complications are just problems dressed up in new clothes.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The {enemy_type} threat near {location} has given {target_npc} a platform to call for unity under their leadership. They're using {quest_threat} as justification to seize emergency authority. If I let them claim credit for defending {location}, {quest_stakes} is theirs by default. I need you to deal with the {enemy_type} stronghold {location_hint} before {target_npc} can organize a response. Clear them out, recover {quest_item} from the ruins, and bring it back as proof that we can protect this settlement better than any speech-maker.
dialogueChunks.outro: Actions speak louder than promises. Let's give {location} something to believe in.

playerResponses.text: A stronghold assault to prove a point? Ambitious. I'm in.
Using a monster threat as a political tool. You're ruthless. I respect it.
I'm a fighter, not a political pawn. Handle your own schemes.
